Meanwhile, 20-year-old Nikola Bartunkova delivered a brilliant performance to advance to her first grass-court quarterfinal, saving five out of six break points in a 6-1, 6-4 win over Elise Mertens.\n[...]\nEala will next face No. 2 seed Elena Rybakina in the second round -- a rematch of their third-round meeting in Rome, which Rybakina won 6-4, 6-3. Bartunkova will take on either No. 1 seed Aryna Sabalenka or Ekaterina Alexandrova as she bids to reach her second tour-level semifinal.\n[...]\nEala improved to 2-0 against Vekic, having also defeated her 4-6, 6-4, 6-4 in the Auckland first round in January.\n[...]\nYoung talents Alexandra Eala and Nikola Bartunkova advanced at the Vanda Pharmaceuticals Berlin Tennis Open with clutch performances against experienced opponents, saving 17 out of 20 break points between them to defeat Donna Vekic and Elise Mertens respectively.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eEala knocks out Queen's champion Vekic in Berlin first round\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.wtatennis.com/videos/4520796/eala-knocks-out-queens-champion-vekic-in-berlin-first-round\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eEala knocks out Queen's champion Vekic in Berlin first round\n\nGo back to the home page\n\n-\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eBy the numbers: Svitolina, Noskova cruise into Berlin quarterfinals\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.wtatennis.com/news/4521583/by-the-numbers-svitolina-noskova-cruise-into-berlin-quarterfinals\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eNo. 6 seed Elina S\n[...]\nolina and No. 8 seed Linda Noskova continued their smooth passages through the Vanda Pharmaceuticals Berlin Tennis Open draw on Thursday, with both needing less than 70 minutes to book their places in the quarterfinals. Noskova defeated qualifier Diane Parry 6-2, 6-2 in 67 minutes, and Svit\n[...]\nfollowed her on Steffi Graf Stadion with a 69-minute 6-3, 6-2 win over wild card Eva Lys.\n[...]\nNoskova advanced to her fourth quarterfinal of 2026, where she will face wild card Paula Badosa.
